Among other things, the moose was proposed to be used in postal distribution, and there was a suggestion to develop a moose-mounted cavalry. The Hungarian monarch was elected by the nobles or land owners (the nobility) from among the candidates, usually within the framework of an assembly known as the Diet or Országgyűlés. This practice was particularly prominent during certain periods of Hungarian history, especially when there was no clear hereditary successor or during interregnums.
